pullRequest.title: feat: add device for Alexa Indoor Air Quality Monitor sensors
pullRequest.body: - Introduce a Home Assistant device to represent the Amazon Indoor Air Quality Monitor (AIAQM) device - Group all AIAQM-related entities (air quality sensors and temperature) under this device - Replace previously unattached AIAQM entities with a properly grouped device representation - Preserve existing entity IDs and behavior for non-AIAQM devices - Filter AIAQM sub-sensors using asset-based friendly names to avoid exposing unsupported capabilities (e.g. PM10: "@type : text") - No new services, controls, or configuration options added Amazon’s _**Indoor Air Quality Monitor**_ reports multiple sensor capabilities via a single appliance record. Previously, these entities were created as independent, unattached sensors in Home Assistant. This change introduces a dedicated synthetic device so all AIAQM sensors are grouped correctly, improving organization without altering existing coordinator or API behavior. The _Amazon Indoor Air Quality Monitor_: - has its own serial number - communicates directly with Amazon - connects via local Wi-Fi connection - is shown in the Alexa app as a standalone device - is not “housed within” a speaker **Creating a dedicated HA device is therefore the correct model.** The Id that's used is tied directly to the hardware serial number: - DeviceInfo.identifiers → (DOMAIN, ) - DeviceInfo.serial_number → - Manufacturer → Amazon - Model → "Indoor Air Quality Monitor" This matches how Echo devices are identified in AMP and gives: - stable identity - clean device registry behavior - no ghost / duplicate devices - correct migration behavior if entities change Fixes #2915 > ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **New Features** * Full support for Amazon Indoor Air Quality Monitor (AIAQM): multi-sensor AQM devices expose distinct temperature and air‑quality sensors and appear as grouped synthetic AQM devices. * **Improvements** * Sensors now attach to synthetic AQM devices with deduplication, validation and consistent unique IDs. * Discovery, monitoring, realtime update routing, stale-device pruning and logging expanded to include entity-backed devices. * **Compatibility** * Parsing and public typings extended to represent AIAQM sensor data. * **Bug Fixes** * Improved unload/teardown to avoid lingering tasks. ✏️ Tip: You can customize this high-level summary in your review settings.

pullRequest.title: fix: Remove deprecated call to get_network_details
pullRequest.body:
Should fix #2940, #2932, #2934, and #2942

This can be a stop gap solution until the API is brought back online (which I doubt it will be).  With this we are losing access to the "Include devices connected via Echo" but the Echo devices should be working again.

I've only performed some basic tests so other functions may also be impacted.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Refactor**
  * Removed deprecated network details retrieval from the data update process to streamline updates.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

pullRequest.title: fix: restore Echo-connected devices
pullRequest.body:
Requires [this PR](https://gitlab.com/keatontaylor/alexapy/-/merge_requests/407) and a bump of alexapy.

I replaced the deprecated call with a GQL query that returns the same fields, but I don't have any device to test it with.

I used skill-connected devices to test these changes and they worked fine, but the reported timestamp was in a different format so I need confirmation from someone with a local device that state reporting works before this gets merged.

Fixes #2956, #2952, #2948, #2945, #2931


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Restored fetching and processing of network details for Alexa devices, improving device and entity updates after startup.
  * Enhanced entity discovery and immediate state availability for Alexa Guard and other entities.

* **Refactor**
  * Simplified processing of network appliance data for more reliable device parsing.
  * Updated timestamp parsing format to enhance capability state validation.

* **Chores**
  * Updated alexapy dependency to version 1.29.7.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

pullRequest.title: ci: harden lokalise translation download
pullRequest.body:
The semantic release workflow was failing when the lokalise translation download step encountered errors, preventing the entire CI pipeline from completing. This change makes the workflow resilient to lokalise failures while preserving the ability to update translations when available.

## Problem
The CI was broken due to the lokalise Docker container or API changes causing the translation download step to fail, which would halt the entire semantic release workflow.

## Solution
Added resilience to the lokalise translation workflow by:

1. **Making lokalise download non-blocking**: Added `continue-on-error: true` to the "Download lokalise translations" step so failures don't stop the workflow
2. **Adding validation logic**: Enhanced the "Move new translations" step to check if lokalise download succeeded before copying files
3. **Preserving existing translations**: If lokalise fails, the workflow proceeds with the existing valid translation files in the repository
4. **Adding informative logging**: Clear messages indicate whether new translations were downloaded or existing ones are being used

## Changes
```yaml
- name: Download lokalise translations
  continue-on-error: true  # Added
  run: |
    docker run -v /tmp/lokalise:/opt/dest lokalise/lokalise-cli-2 ...

- name: Move new translations
  continue-on-error: true  # Added
  run: |
    if [ -d "/tmp/lokalise/locale" ] && [ "$(ls -A /tmp/lokalise/locale 2>/dev/null)" ]; then
      echo "Lokalise translations downloaded successfully, updating translation files..."
      cp /tmp/lokalise/locale/* .../translations/
    else
      echo "Lokalise download failed or no translations found, proceeding with existing translations..."
    fi
```

## Testing
- All existing tests pass
- Manual simulation confirms correct behavior in all scenarios:
  - Lokalise download fails completely → uses existing translations
  - Lokalise creates directory but no files → uses existing translations  
  - Lokalise succeeds with files → copies new translations
- All 18 existing translation files remain valid JSON
- YAML syntax validation passes

This ensures the semantic release workflow can complete successfully even when lokalise is unavailable, addressing both requirements from the issue: CI resilience and preservation of existing valid data.

Fixes #2962.

>

pullRequest.title: test: add core utility functions with TimeoutException typo fix
pullRequest.body:
This PR significantly expands the automated testing coverage for the alexa_media_player integration by adding comprehensive tests for core utility functions and components, and fixes a typo in the TimeoutException class.

## Changes Made

### Test Coverage Expansion
- **Increased from 5 to 73 tests** (1360% increase)
- **Overall code coverage improved to 11%** with targeted 100% coverage of key utility modules

### Bug Fix
- **Fixed typo in TimeoutException**: Corrected "Timeour exception" to "Timeout exception" in the exception message format

### New Test Modules Added

#### `tests/test_exceptions.py`
- Complete test coverage for all custom exception classes
- Tests exception initialization, inheritance, and proper error message formatting
- Validates `LoginInvalidException` attempts tracking functionality

#### `tests/test_alexa_entity.py` 
- Comprehensive testing of utility functions in `alexa_entity.py`:
  - `has_capability()` - Tests Alexa device capability detection with various scenarios
  - `is_hue_v1()` - Validates Philips Hue v1 device identification
  - `is_skill()` - Tests skill-based device detection
  - `is_known_ha_bridge()` - Validates Home Assistant bridge detection
  - `is_local()` - Tests local device detection including zigbee pattern matching

#### `tests/test_helpers.py` (Extended)
- Added extensive testing for `add_devices()` function:
  - Device filtering with include/exclude lists
  - Error handling for entity conflicts and exceptions
  - Success and failure scenarios
  - Edge cases with empty device lists

#### `tests/test_const.py`
- Validation of all configuration constants and values
- Ensures proper format and consistency of URLs, timeouts, and component lists
- Verifies semantic versioning compliance

## Benefits

1. **Improved Reliability**: Core utility functions now have comprehensive test coverage
2. **Regression Prevention**: Changes to key functions will be caught by automated tests
3. **Documentation**: Tests serve as living documentation of expected behavior
4. **CI Integration**: Existing CI pipeline will now catch more potential issues
5. **Maintainability**: Easier to refactor with confidence knowing tests will catch breaking changes
6. **Bug Prevention**: Fixed existing typo that could cause confusion in error messages

## Testing Strategy

The tests focus on:
- **Isolated unit testing** of utility functions without complex Home Assistant setup
- **Edge case coverage** including error conditions and invalid inputs
- **Behavioral validation** ensuring functions work as documented
- **Exception handling** testing both success and failure paths

All tests are designed to be fast, reliable, and independent of external services.

Fixes #2972.

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

💬 Share your feedback on Copilot coding agent for the chance to win a $200 gift card! Click [here](https://survey.alchemer.com/s3/8343779/Copilot-Coding-agent) to start the survey.

pullRequest.title: docs: add GitHub Copilot instructions
pullRequest.body:
This PR creates comprehensive GitHub Copilot instructions for the alexa_media_player repository to help developers quickly understand how to build, test, and work with this Home Assistant custom component.

## What's Added

The `.github/copilot-instructions.md` file provides exhaustively validated instructions covering:

**Setup and Dependencies:**
- Python 3.13 installation requirements with fallback strategies for Python 3.12
- Poetry-based dependency management with pip fallbacks for network-restricted environments
- Core dependencies: alexapy, aiohttp, packaging, wrapt, async_timeout

**Development Workflow:**
- Validated linting tools with exact timing: black (0.8s), isort (0.2s), yamllint (0.1s), codespell (0.2s)
- Pre-commit hook setup with network dependency warnings
- Testing infrastructure using pytest-homeassistant-custom-component
- CI/CD integration with GitHub Actions workflows

**Project Context:**
- Home Assistant custom component architecture (~8,500 lines Python, 19+ translations)
- Component structure and key files (media_player.py, config_flow.py, const.py)
- Integration with HACS and Home Assistant validation tools
- Dependency on unofficial Amazon Alexa API

**Comprehensive Validation:**
All commands and timing recommendations were tested and validated. The instructions include "NEVER CANCEL" warnings for long-running operations with specific timeout values:
- Poetry operations: 10+ minute timeouts
- Pre-commit hooks: 15+ minute timeouts  
- CI builds: 10-15 minute timeouts

**Troubleshooting:**
- Python version conflicts and workarounds
- Network connectivity issues with Poetry and pre-commit
- Home Assistant dependency complexity for testing
- Common CI/CD failures and solutions

## Why This Helps

These instructions enable any developer to:
1. Set up the development environment correctly on first try
2. Understand the project structure and key components immediately  
3. Run validated linting and testing workflows without trial-and-error
4. Avoid common pitfalls with dependency management and network issues
5. Work effectively with Home Assistant integration requirements

The instructions follow an imperative tone throughout ("Run this command", "Do not do this") and prioritize working, validated commands over verbose explanations.

Fixes #2996.

## Summary by CodeRabbit

pullRequest.title: fix: Handle CancelledError explicitly in update_last_called
pullRequest.body:
Explicitly handle asyncio.CancelledError in update_last_called to allow intentional task cancellation (e.g., probe rescheduling or shutdown) to exit quietly.

With the new alexapy change propagating CancelledError, this ensures:
- Debounced probe cancellation does not surface as task noise
- Cancellation is not misinterpreted as an API failure
- Normal asyncio semantics are preserved

No functional changes to last_called behavior.

Fixes: #3206 
Requires: alexapy [MR!450: fix: Propagate CancelledError; avoid logging cancellation as timeout](https://gitlab.com/keatontaylor/alexapy/-/merge_requests/450)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Improved cancellation handling for background probe tasks to avoid lingering operations and reduce unexpected interruptions

* **Performance**
  * Reduced device fetch timeout from 20 seconds to 10 seconds for faster responses

* **General Improvements**
  * Enhanced debug logging and diagnostics for better troubleshooting and visibility
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

pullRequest.title: ci: skip autocloser for contributor and bot issues
pullRequest.body:
The autocloser was closing all issues that didn't follow templates, including those from contributors and bots needed for Copilot usage.

## Changes

- Added conditional to `.github/workflows/autocloser.yaml` to skip the issue-closer action for users with contributor associations (OWNER, MEMBER, CONTRIBUTOR, COLLABORATOR)
- Added check to exclude bot accounts (coderabbitai, copilot, github-actions[bot], dependabot[bot])
- Condition filters to `issues` events only and checks `author_association` and `user.login` via GitHub context

```yaml
if: |
  github.event_name == 'issues' &&
  !contains(fromJSON('["OWNER", "MEMBER", "CONTRIBUTOR", "COLLABORATOR"]'), github.event.issue.author_association) &&
  !contains(fromJSON('["coderabbitai", "copilot", "github-actions[bot]", "dependabot[bot]"]'), github.event.issue.user.login)
```

Non-contributors and non-bot accounts continue to have issues auto-closed if templates aren't followed.

pullRequest.title: ci: modernize issue templates and simplify workflows
pullRequest.body:
Replaces markdown issue templates with GitHub's YAML issue forms to provide structured data collection and validation.

## Changes

**Issue Templates**
- Converted `bug_report.md` → `bug_report.yml` with 9 form fields (4 required, 5 optional)
  - Combined three version input fields into one textarea for better UX
  - Changed Amazon Domain to dropdown with 14 common options
  - Added separate text input field for "Other" Amazon domain (GitHub dropdowns don't support conditional free-form text)
  - **Debug logs now optional** with guidance on when they're most helpful (login issues, device discovery, errors)
  - Supports multiple log submission methods: paste excerpts, drag-and-drop files, or pastebin links
  - Generic version placeholders (x.x.x) to avoid maintenance overhead
- Converted `feature_request.md` → `feature_request.yml` with 4 form fields (2 required, 2 optional)
- Auto-apply labels: "bug" and "feature"
- Pre-fill titles with "[Bug]: " and "[Feature]: " prefixes
- Fixed typo: "homeasssistant" → "Home Assistant"

**Issue Template Configuration**
- `blank_issues_enabled: false` - prevents non-contributors from bypassing templates via UI
- Added contact link for contributors to create free-form issues via direct URL (`/issues/new`)
- Contributors with write access can still create free-form issues when needed (e.g., for Copilot, bots, automated reports)

**CI Workflows**
- Removed `autocloser.yaml` - no longer needed as GitHub natively enforces required fields in public repos
- Simplified `moderate.yaml` - now focuses solely on duplicate detection

## Key Improvements

- Required fields enforced at submission time by GitHub
- More flexible form with fewer required fields (4 vs original 7) - reduces friction for valid reports
- Combined version field improves user experience
- Amazon Domain dropdown ensures consistent data, with optional text field for unlisted domains
- 2SV dropdown ensures consistent "Yes"/"No" values vs freeform text
- Optional logs reduce friction while providing clear guidance on when to include them
- Supports multiple log submission methods for user flexibility
- Contributors can create free-form issues via direct link while non-contributors must use templates
- Structured data enables automated processing
- Reduced CI maintenance by removing unnecessary workflow
- Generic placeholders reduce maintenance burden

## Notes

- GitHub YAML forms do not support dynamically pulling values from repository data (like released versions) without additional automation complexity. The textarea approach for versions keeps it simple and flexible.
- GitHub dropdown fields don't allow conditional free-form text input. A workaround is provided via a separate optional text field for users to specify unlisted Amazon domains when selecting "Other".
- Debug logs are optional to accommodate various bug report types (UI issues, documentation problems, etc.) while still encouraging logs when they're helpful for debugging functional issues.
- GitHub doesn't support role-based restrictions for `blank_issues_enabled`. With it set to `false`, all users are prevented from using the blank issue option in the UI, but contributors with write access can still create free-form issues via the direct URL (added as a contact link), GitHub API, or by creating issues from code/comments/project boards.

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Modernize bug report and issue templates</issue_title>
> <issue_description>We should update the bug report and issue templates to use more modern form fields as opposed to the text only. CI should be updated to recognize these templates.</issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
>

> </comments>
> 


</details>

pullRequest.title: feat: add stricter Matter bridge detection
pullRequest.body:
It's been determined that the Home-Assistant-Matter-Hub now exposes most entities with their real HA manufacturer name with fallback to `tobst4r` when unavailable. This leads to AMP replicating those entities back into Home Assistant when `extended_entity_discovery` is enabled.

This addresses that change in behaviour by checking  "Alexa.Matter.NodeOperationalCredentials.FabricManagement" or "Alexa.Commissionable" thereby preventing the loop.

- Fixes #3302

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Improved Matter bridge hub detection to more reliably identify compatible bridges and reduce false positives, improving device identification and integration stability across setups.

<sub>✏️ Tip: You can customize this high-level summary in your review settings.</sub>
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

The bridge is now resolved using the `applianceId` prefix (before `#`), restoring correct exclusion behavior.

pullRequest.title: fix: preserve notification sensor unique_ids on upgrade (#3345)
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
- Restore original `f"next {self._type}"` name parameter in AlarmSensor, TimerSensor, and ReminderSensor constructors
- PR #3339 changed these from `"next Alarm"` to `"Next alarm"`, altering `_attr_unique_id` and causing HA to create duplicate entities with `_2` suffix on upgrade from pre-5.12.0
- With `has_entity_name=True` + `translation_key`, the `name` parameter only affects `unique_id`, not the displayed name

Fixes #3345

## Test plan

- [x] Deployed buggy version → confirmed `_3` suffixed duplicates appear in entity registry
- [x] Deployed fix → confirmed original entities reused, zero duplicates

pullRequest.title: docs: clarify cached device mutation contract
pullRequest.body:
Issue identified during review of PR https://github.com/alandtse/alexa_media_player/pull/3390
Reported by: https://github.com/coderabbitai
Requested by: @alandtse

Description:
In custom_components/alexa_media/__init__.py around lines 588-712, the DataCache documentation states it "stores a direct reference (not a copy)" and warns callers not to mutate. However, lines 919-946 mutate individual device dicts in-place (adding bluetooth_state, locale, dnd, etc.), which modifies the cached reference.

Current behavior:
The code intentionally mutates cached references, which contradicts the cache's documented contract.

Suggested improvements:
Either:

Deep-copy on cache retrieval: devices = copy.deepcopy(cached_devices)
Add a clear comment acknowledging that mutation is intentional in this specific case
Impact:
Nitpick - Works correctly in practice because mutations overwrite with fresh data each cycle, but violates documented contract.

Reference:

PR: https://github.com/alandtse/alexa_media_player/pull/3390
File: custom_components/alexa_media/init.py:588-712

Fixes: #3395 

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Documentation**
  * Clarified how cached device data is handled and guidance on treating cached values as read-only unless explicitly owned and mutated.

* **Chores**
  * Added explanatory comments noting that device records are enriched in-place during refresh cycles.

Note: No user-facing changes in this release.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

pullRequest.title: fix: link coordinator to config entry
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #3409 

## Summary
Pass the config entry explicitly when `AlexaMediaCoordinator` initializes `DataUpdateCoordinator`.

## Why
After successful login / reauthentication, the integration proceeds into normal setup and creates the coordinator from `runtime_data`. That runtime data already contains `config_entry`, but the coordinator currently does not forward it to `DataUpdateCoordinator`.

This can surface as the Home Assistant error: `Detected code that uses async_config_entry_first_refresh, which is only supported for coordinators with a config entry`.

## Change
```diff
 super().__init__(
     hass,
     _LOGGER,
     name=DOMAIN,
+    config_entry=runtime_data.config_entry if runtime_data else None,
     update_method=update_method,
     update_interval=update_interval,
     request_refresh_debouncer=debouncer,
 )
```

## Validation
- `python3 -m py_compile custom_components/alexa_media/coordinator.py`

## References
- Home Assistant docs: Coordinated, single API poll for data for all entities  
  https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/integration_fetching_data/#coordinated-single-api-poll-for-data-for-all-entities
- Home Assistant docs: Config entries  
  https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/config_entries_index/
- Home Assistant source: `DataUpdateCoordinator` constructor accepts `config_entry`  
  https://github.com/home-assistant/core/blob/dev/homeassistant/helpers/update_coordinator.py#L75-L112
- Home Assistant source: `async_config_entry_first_refresh()` requires a coordinator with a config entry  
  https://github.com/home-assistant/core/blob/dev/homeassistant/helpers/update_coordinator.py#L321-L333

## Related
- Fixes the post-login coordinator initialization issue reported in the accompanying bug report.


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Coordinator initialization now correctly ties configuration entries to coordinator instances, improving stability and reliability of periodic data updates and refresh behavior. This reduces mismatches between runtime configuration and update scheduling, resulting in more consistent synchronization of device data and fewer update-related issues.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

pullRequest.title: fix: refine last_called with activity-correlated push events
pullRequest.body:
Refines last_called handling to trigger the customer-history probe from activity-correlated push events (PUSH_VOLUME_CHANGE and PUSH_EQUALIZER_STATE_CHANGE) instead of connection-based events.

The probe worker:
- Debounces rapid push bursts
- Coalesces duplicate triggers
- Retries briefly when history is temporarily stale
- Applies bounded backoff for 429/login/connection errors

Also improves media_player and notify handling to ensure:
- last_called only updates when the active device actually changes
- Timestamp comparisons are robust
- Notify targets consistently reflect the newest last_called entity

No changes to the entity model or coordinator behavior.

Fixes: #3412 

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Per-account near-real-time "last called" flow with queued activity, a single debounced background probe worker, coalescing of related pushes, and exposure of last_called_response on device entities; event emission deferred for reliable dispatch.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Improved rate-limit/backoff timing, retry and error handling; clearer timestamp/JSON parsing; distinct handling of cancels, rate-limits and connection/login errors; safer mapping re-registration and more robust startup/shutdown behavior.

* **Chores**
  * Centralized tuning constants and CI updates to preserve and upload test coverage artifacts.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

pullRequest.title: refactor: move push health magic numbers to const.py
pullRequest.body:
Extract the three literals into named constants in const.py:

- HTTP2_ERROR_THRESHOLD = 5
- LAST_PUSH_INACTIVITY_SECONDS = 600.0
- LAST_PING_MAX_AGE_SECONDS = 900.0

and replace the literals in the helper functions with those constants.

Fixes #3419 

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Refactor**
  * Internal improvements to push connection health monitoring configuration.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
